title: oxytropis sordida
intro: Oxytropis sordida, also known as the rough-leaf locoweed, is a perennial plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Oxytropis sordida include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically used in moderation due to its potential toxicity when consumed in large quantities.
